title: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN SHRIMPS OR PRAWNS

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN SHRIMPS OR PRAWNS
sections.summary: Title and identification of the Codex Standard for Quick Frozen Shrimps or Prawns (CODEX STAN 92-1981), adopted 1981, revised 1995 and 2017, and amended 2011, 2013, 2014.
Applies to quick frozen raw, partially cooked, or fully cooked shrimps or prawns, whether peeled or unpeeled.

Defines product families and processing/presentation requirements: specifies permitted species families, rapid freezing to reach −18°C at the thermal centre with maintenance of deep-frozen conditions to minimize dehydration and oxidation, and allows any presentation provided labelling is adequate and pack contents meet the standard.

Defines quick frozen shrimp as product from families Penaeidae, Pandalidae, Crangonidae, and Palaemonidae and prohibits mixing genera in a pack while allowing mixtures of species within the same genus with similar sensory properties.

Requires potable or clean sea-water for cooking and cooling, rapid passage through the crystallization range in appropriate freezing equipment, attainment of −18°C or colder at the thermal centre after stabilization, continuous deep-freezing during distribution, and processing/packaging to minimize dehydration and oxidation.
Permits any product presentation if it meets the standard and is adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers; shrimp may be packed by count per unit of weight or per package.

Specifies essential composition and quality factors including use of sound shrimp fit for sale fresh, glazing water standards, and ingredient quality, and states that product compliance is determined by lot examinations and specified analytical methods.
Requires quick frozen shrimp be prepared from sound shrimp of a quality fit for sale as fresh for human consumption.

If glazed, glazing water or glazing solutions must be potable or clean sea-water meeting the microbiological standards equivalent to WHO drinking-water guidelines and be free from objectionable substances.
Requires the packing medium and all other ingredients to be food-grade and to conform to all applicable Codex standards.
States that products shall meet the standard when lots examined per Section 9 comply with the provisions of Section 8, using the examination methods given in Section 7.

Permits acidity regulators, antioxidants, colours, humectants and preservatives when used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives for food category 09.2.1 (frozen fish and related products) and its parent categories.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene codes (General Principles of Food Hygiene, Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products, Code for Quick Frozen Foods, etc.) and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established under Codex Principles and Guidelines for Microbiological Criteria.
Section heading stating that additional specific labelling provisions supplement the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods.

Requires the label to declare the product name as "shrimps" or "prawns" per local usage, include descriptive presentation near the name, allow non-misleading trade names, indicate cooked/partially cooked/raw status, state if glazed with sea-water, use "quick frozen" (or "frozen" where customary), and include instructions to maintain quality during transport, storage and distribution.
Requires the declared net contents for glazed products to exclude the weight of the glaze.
Requires the label to state that the product shall be stored at −18°C or colder.

Allows required information to appear on the non-retail container or accompanying documents but mandates that the name of the food, lot identification, name and address, and storage instructions always appear on the container (lot identification and name/address may be replaced by an identification mark clearly linked to accompanying documents).

Contains procedures for sampling, examination and analyses of frozen shrimp/prawn lots including lot sampling (AQL 6.5; sample unit = primary container or ≥1 kg for IQF), sensory/physical assessment by trained personnel, net weight determination (including detailed de-glazing/weighing for glazed products), count determination, thawing and specified cooking methods and related examination steps.

Requires lot sampling for end‑product examination per Section 3.3 using an appropriate sampling plan with AQL 6.5 (sample unit = primary container or at least 1 kg for individually quick frozen products) and specifies that net‑weight sampling meet criteria established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

Mandates that sensory and physical examinations be conducted by personnel trained in such assessments and follow the procedures in Sections 7.3–7.6, Annex A and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31‑1999).

Specifies net weight determination: for non‑glazed products weigh each sample unit in the frozen state, and for glazed products prescribes a de‑glazing, thawing, sieving and weighing procedure (including sieve aperture 2.8 mm or 2.38 mm, sieve diameters 20 cm for ≤500 g and 30 cm for >500 g, and a 2‑minute drain) to calculate net content.

sections.section: 7.3.1 Determination of net weight of products not covered by glaze
sections.summary: States that the net weight (exclusive of packaging) of each sample unit representing a lot must be determined in the frozen state.

sections.section: 7.3.2 Determination of net weight of products covered by glaze
sections.summary: Heading only for determination of net weight of glazed products; the detailed de‑glazing and weighing procedure is provided in the following 'Procedure' subsection.

Provides stepwise procedure for determining net weight of glazed quick‑frozen shrimp/prawns: open immediately after removal from cold storage; for raw product rinse in room‑temperature water at ~25 L/min, for cooked product immerse in 27°C water equal to 8× declared weight until ice melts (turn block if necessary); weigh a dry clean sieve (2.8 mm or 2.38 mm aperture) of 20 cm diameter for ≤500 g or 30 cm for >500 g; remove visible glaze, drain on a ~20° inclined sieve for two minutes, weigh and subtract sieve mass to obtain net content.

When a count is declared on the label, determine count by counting shrimp in the container or a representative sample and dividing the count by the actual de‑glazed weight to obtain count per unit weight.

Specifies thawing by enclosing the sample unit in a film bag and immersing in water at room temperature (≤35°C), gently squeezing occasionally until no hard core or ice crystals remain to avoid damaging shrimp texture.

Prescribes cooking methods to reach an internal temperature of 65–70°C and warns not to overcook, noting cooking times vary by size and should be established by prior experimentation; describes baking, steaming, boil‑in‑bag and microwave procedures and advises checking for odors from plastics when used.

Declares a sample unit defective if it exhibits any of the defined properties and lists the defect categories to be used (deep dehydration, foreign matter, odour/flavour, discolouration) with criteria provided in subsections.

Defines deep dehydration as either >10% of the shrimp weight or >10% of the surface area of a block showing excessive moisture loss (white/yellow abnormalities) that penetrate below the surface and cannot be removed by scraping without unduly affecting appearance.

Defines foreign matter as any material not derived from shrimp that does not pose a human‑health threat but is readily recognized without magnification (or detectable by methods including magnification) at levels indicating non‑compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.
Classifies shrimp with persistent, distinct objectionable odours or flavours indicative of decomposition, rancidity or of feed as defective.
Defines discolouration as distinct blackening or green or yellow discoloration affecting more than 10% of the surface area of individual shrimp and impacting more than 25% of the sample unit.

Specifies lot acceptance criteria: total defectives per Section 8 and sample units not meeting count designation per Section 2.3 must not exceed the acceptance number (c) of the sampling plan with AQL 6.5, the average net weight of sample units must be at least the declared weight without unreasonable shortage in any container, and Sections 4, 5 and 6 on Food Additives, Hygiene and Labelling must be met.

Provides a stepwise sensory and physical examination checklist: determine net weight per Section 7.3 (de‑glaze as required); examine frozen shrimp for dehydration and quantify affected percentage; thaw per Section 7.5 and inspect each shrimp for foreign matter/presentation defects and measure affected weight; verify count per Section 7.4; assess odour and discolouration and, if odour/flavour is inconclusive, cook a 100–200 g portion per Section 7.6 to confirm.
ANNEX A is presented as a heading only with no substantive text in this extract.
